title: uuf2str
html:
uuf2str

  • Last UpdatedJan 04, 2023
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Get a pointer to the string data in a basic parameter or entity field.

long uuf2str (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, UCHAR** pStr);

Parameters

hOpr
The handle to the operation or entity
seqNr
The sequence number of the parameter or entity field (sequence numbers start with 1)
pStr
(Output) A pointer to the string data

Return Values

For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic.

Use

Library: ucall

Description

uuf2str returns (by reference) a pointer to the string data of a basic parameter or entity field.

The data pointed to by pStr is in external format. If you need to access the data in Uniface format, use uprmgets rather than uuf2str.

As with all functions that return a pointer by reference, the calling program is responsible for freeing the allocated memory. So you must call unifree to free the data. Not doing so might result in a memory leak.

Example: uuf2str, unifree, and ustr2uf

The following code calls uuf2str to retrieve string data from an output parameter of one service. It then modifies the string and calls ustr2uf to pass it as an input parameter to another service. Note that, because we want to access and modify the string, we cannot use the detach/attach mechanism here. The operations OP1 and OP2 are both located in the service TESTSVC, and are defined as follows:

operation op1
params
string msg : inout;
endparams
msg = "the quick brow fox jumps over the lazy dog"
end

operation op2
params
string msg : in;
endparams
putmess msg
end

And here is the 3GL routine:

#include <h3gl.h>
#include <ctype.h>

void get_and_put ()
{
UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */
UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */
long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */
UCHAR* pMsg; /* Pointer to string parameter data */
UCHAR* p; /* Work pointer */

ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */
/* Instantiate service TESTSVC */
uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0,
UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);
uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP1", 0, &hOp1); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */
uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP2", 0, &hOp2); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */

uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP1 */
uuf2str(hOp1, 1, &pMsg); /* Get a pointer to the string */

for ( p=pMsg; *p; p++ ) *p = toupper(*p);/* Convert string to uppercase*/
ustr2uf(hOp2, 1, pMsg); /* Copy string to OP2 input parameter */
uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP2 */

uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */
unifree (hOp1, pMsg); /* Free the string data */
ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free OP1 operation handle */
ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free OP2 operation handle */
ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */
ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */
}
Related Links
  • Call-In API Return Codes

    Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.

  • uprmgets

    Return Uniface-formatted data from a basic parameter or entity field.

  • ustr2uf

    Copy string data from a program buffer to a basic parameter or entity field.
